page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-176 is a compute-optimized server instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 176 virtual CPUs mapped to 88 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8481C processor operating at 2.7 GHz. The system allocates 352 GB of dedicated system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or onboard GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and CPU-bound execution.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor incorporates advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). These hardware-level features accelerate matrix multiplication and vector operations, which are reflected in top-tier benchmark results for cryptographic processing and extended instructions. The CPU cache configuration consists of 48 KB L1 data cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and a large 105 MB L3 cache, facilitating rapid data access for highly parallelized workloads.

In benchmark evaluations, the c3-highcpu-176 demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core tests and PassMark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, achieving top-tier status in both small-block cached and large-block uncached read operations. However, single-core performance remains in the average tier, indicating that the instance is optimized for highly parallelized execution rather than single-threaded speed. Additionally, while database operations and Redis SET operations perform strongly, the lack of local storage requires network-based storage solutions, which may introduce latency tradeoffs.

Given its high vCPU density and specialized instruction sets, this server is designed for compute-heavy workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, image processing, and static web serving. It also shows strong capability in CPU-based LLM inference, particularly for prompt processing. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c3-highcpu-176 offers high compute density per resource for parallel processing tasks, though the low memory-to-vCPU ratio makes it less suitable for memory-intensive in-memory databases.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-176 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor with 176 vCPUs (88 physical cores) running at 2.7 GHz and 352 GB of dedicated RAM. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this instance relies on network storage and focuses entirely on raw CPU execution. It features advanced hardware extensions like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which drive top-tier multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic execution in benchmark testing. While single-core performance is average, its massive parallel processing capacity yields strong results in software compilation, ray tracing, and static web serving. The c3-highcpu-176 is highly cost-efficient for compute-dense, parallel workloads, though its 2.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio presents a tradeoff for memory-bound applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-176 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 176 vCPUs, 352 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor. It delivers top-tier multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-176 is a compute-optimized server with 176 vCPUs and 352 GB RAM, built for highly parallelized workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute server featuring 176 virtual cores and advanced matrix extensions for parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 176 vCPUs and 352 GB of dedicated RAM powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 extensions for accelerated matrix and vector operations.
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and high-bandwidth memory read/write speeds.
Optimized for parallel workloads including software compilation, ray tracing, and web serving.
No local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring network-attached storage.
